// Additional weak stubs for callbacks declared in abi.h but not implemented above.
-// dymStatSinkHandle implements shared.StatSinkHandle. Today it only carries
-// logging. It is kept as a struct so we can add fields (worker index, shared
-// data) without changing the interface contract.
+// dymStatSinkHandle implements shared.StatSinkHandle. It carries the Envoy-side
+// configuration pointer used to define and set gauges and, lazily, a scheduler
+// whose committed tasks run on the main thread.
+		// The scheduler is created lazily and should never be nil in
+		// practice. But it will be nil in mock tests.
// dymMetricSnapshot implements shared.MetricSnapshot by delegating to the
// Envoy callbacks. It is a short-lived value constructed once per OnFlush call
// and discarded when OnFlush returns. The underlying Envoy pointer is only
handle.Log(shared.LogLevelWarn, "Failed to load stats sink configuration: %v", err)
+	// Drop the scheduler reference so its finalizer can release the Envoy-side resource. Envoy never

+	if wrapper == nil || wrapper.handle == nil || wrapper.handle.scheduler == nil {
-// Fill decodes a single value into buf, growing it and retrying when the value did not fit. fill
-// writes up to cap(buf) bytes and returns the full size of the value and whether it exists. On
-// success the returned slice holds exactly the bytes written. When the value does not exist, buf is
-// returned unchanged with false. The size is stable during a flush, so this converges in at most
-// two iterations. Pass buf[:0] and assign the result back to reuse the allocation.
+// Fill decodes a single value into buf, growing it and retrying when the value did not fit. The
+// fill callback writes up to cap(buf) bytes and returns the full size of the value and whether it
+// exists. On success the returned slice holds exactly the bytes written. When the value does not
+// exist, buf is returned unchanged with false. The size is stable during a flush, so this
+// converges in at most two iterations. Pass buf[:0] and assign the result back to reuse the

-// StatSinkHandle is passed to StatSink methods. It currently exposes only

+// StatSinkHandle is passed to the StatSinkConfigFactory and gives a sink access
+// to host services. It provides logging, gauge definition and updates, and
+// scheduling work back onto the main thread.
+	// Log writes a message to Envoy's logger at the given level.
+	// DefineGauge creates a gauge with the given name and returns its ID. It must
+	// be called while the sink is being created, from StatSinkConfigFactory.Create.
+	// Defining a gauge afterwards returns MetricsFrozen.
+	// SetGauge sets a gauge previously defined with DefineGauge to value. It must

+	// GetScheduler returns a scheduler whose scheduled functions run on the main

/// The `name` is the value of `sink_name` from the `dynamic_modules` stats sink configuration,
/// allowing a single module to dispatch to different sink implementations. The `config` is the
-/// raw bytes from the `sink_config` field. Returning `None` causes Envoy to reject the stats
+/// raw bytes from the `sink_config` field. The `envoy_config` handle is used to define gauges and

/// The global factory function for stats sinks. This is set via the `stat_sink:` arm of
// None should result in a null pointer (for example an unknown sink name).
+  let snapshot = stats_sink::MetricSnapshot::new(&mut dummy as *mut _ as *mut std::ffi::c_void);
+  // The name buffer is too small but the value buffer already fits, so only the name is grown and

+// Recording stubs and shared state for the stats sink gauge and scheduler callbacks. Tests that
+// exercise these callbacks must hold STAT_SINK_TEST_LOCK and call reset_stat_sink_stubs() so the
+// shared state is deterministic despite the parallel test runner.
+  let name = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(name.ptr as *const u8, name.length) };
+  unsafe { *gauge_id_ptr = names.len() }; // 1-based ID, mirroring the host.
+  // Allocate a token so commit and delete receive a real, non-null pointer just like the host.
+  // This drives only the read-only snapshot getter stubs, not the shared SS_* state, so unlike the
+  // gauge and scheduler tests below it does not need STAT_SINK_TEST_LOCK.
+  let snapshot = stats_sink::MetricSnapshot::new(&mut dummy as *mut _ as *mut std::ffi::c_void);
+  // The owned snapshot is Send, so it can be moved to another thread for aggregation.
